action.reasoning: Observation: The larger model (hidden=256, layers=3, dropout=0.2) trained for 20 epochs achieved mae_60min=4.2428, improving over the 4.4535 baseline. Training loss still declining (0.59) and validation standard-L1 is flat (~0.37), indicating no overfitting and room for more capacity. Analysis: The network is underfitting; we can increase width/depth, add an adaptive adjacency matrix, use gated temporal convolutions (GLU), aggregate multi-layer skip connections, and more aggressively weight the later time steps in the loss (1x→3x). Training throughput is very high (~18s/epoch), so a 40-epoch run with a 512×4 model (~9M params) will take ~12-15 minutes—well within budget. Decision: Rewrite model.py with adaptive graph, GLU, skip connections; update train.py with hidden=512, layers=4, dropout=0.3, weight decay=5e-4, input noise augmentation, stronger horizon weighting, and 40 epochs. Then submit eval.
